records.ety: [OE. on live, AS. on līfe in life; līfe being dat. of līf life. See Life, and cf. Live, a.]

defs: Having life, in opposition to dead; living; being in a state in which the organs perform their functions; as, an animal or a plant which is alive.
In a state of action; in force or operation; unextinguished; unexpired; existent; as, to keep the fire alive; to keep the affections alive.
Exhibiting the activity and motion of many living beings; swarming; thronged.
quotes: The Boyne, for a quarter of a mile, was alive with muskets and green boughs.

defs: Having susceptibility; easily impressed; having lively feelings, as opposed to apathy; sensitive.
Of all living (by way of emphasis).
quotes: Northumberland was the proudest man alive.
notes: Used colloquially as an intensive; as, man alive!
☞ Alive always follows the noun which it qualifies.
